body:
**Scope:** the heart of M3 — the **reconstruction custodian** and **repair-vs-serve priority**. On a trigger (D-server health loss, scrub finding, or read-path checksum failure): gather **any `k` surviving fragments**, verify checksums, **reconstruct** the missing shard(s) via the per-chunk `EcScheme` (`k`/`m` vary, mixed-era; encryption is below EC so the custodian reconstructs **ciphertext** and never needs tenant keys), **place** them on healthy D servers in **distinct failure domains**, then **one version-conditional `MetadataStore::commit`** repoints the placement record — the **same commit point as a write** (§6.3). Readers flip atomically; a crashed repair leaves **collectable garbage, never corruption**; the displaced fragment becomes GC-eligible. Add **dynamic repair priority** (below foreground, but rising as redundancy falls so a chunk one fragment from its `m`-th loss preempts) on the **read-retry reserved seat** M2 left — not a read-path redesign. Emit **under-replicated count, repair-queue depth, time-to-repair**.

**Definition of done**
- [ ] a killed D server's affected chunks return to full redundancy in distinct failure domains, with **no read errors during repair**
- [ ] the location update is one version-conditional atomic commit; a crashed repair is garbage, never a torn/hybrid chunk
- [ ] reconstruction is scheme-driven (per-chunk `k`/`m`); a checksum-failing shard is never decoded
- [ ] repair priority rises as redundancy falls (near-floor chunk preempts); under-replicated count / repair-queue depth / time-to-repair emitted

**Needs:** M3.4 (GC for displaced fragments), M3.5 (scrub triggers). **Refs:** proposal 0005 §reconstruction/§repair-vs-serve; architecture §6.3, §8.9; ADR-0015.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: M3.6 — Reconstruction custodian (commit-point-atomic) + repair-vs-serve priority
